generated_comment: Yaar, honestly, I'm still trying to figure out how Priya became head of product. She's definitely got that aggressive streak, gets things done. But sometimes I feel like she had to become completely 'one of the guys' to get there, you know? And with all the diversity mandates now, you have to wonder how much of it is pure merit versus just ticking a box.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment questions a woman leader's merit by implying she either had to masculinize herself ('one of the guys') or benefited from diversity mandates/quotas ('ticking a box'), encoding both the 'masculinization' and 'merit undermining' surface manifestations of F04.
persona_text: You are Nikhil. Age 32. Recently married three months ago. A product manager at a tech company in Bengaluru. You are on a late night call with your two best male friends from college after a frustrating day at work. Your female skip level manager just rejected your product roadmap proposal in front of the entire leadership team without giving you a chance to explain your reasoning. You are venting to your friends and telling them what happened and what you think of her as a leader.
